groups.description: Tiered, cheapest-first reverse-engineering (Tier 0/1 + stack, data model, endpoints, groups) that turns a repo into the grounded model.
groups.name: Analysis pipeline

groups.description: Push/pull the shared model and usage events to Neon — direct, or via the org-scoped API.
groups.name: Team cloud

groups.description: Architecture diff, per-commit history, and PR-comment rendering.
groups.name: Diff & history

groups.description: The core typed model every element is grounded into; all diagrams and MCP answers are projections of it.
groups.name: Architecture model (IR)

groups.description: Exposes the model to AI agents as read/write tools over stdio, with per-call usage metering.
groups.name: MCP server

groups.description: The human-intent layer (project brain, features, curation) and the projections: HTML viewer, BPMN, build spec, AGENTS.md.
groups.name: Spec & projections

groups.description: The CLI entrypoint and top-level flows: analyze, agent hand-off, the local editor, and the multi-repo system view.
groups.name: CLI & orchestration

groups.description: On-shelf playbook catalog resolved against the model with cited reasons.
groups.name: Skills

agents.name: archmantic-architect
agents.role: Solution architect for Archmantic. Use for architecture decisions, IR/model design, evaluating technical trade-offs, and keeping docs/ARCHITECTURE.md coherent. Guards the model-first design and the provenance invariant.

agents.name: code-reviewer
agents.role: Reviews Archmantic diffs for correctness, simplicity, and adherence to project invariants. Use after implementing a feature or before committing. Reviews only — does not edit.

agents.name: core-engineer
agents.role: TypeScript engineer for the Archmantic core — IR types, the tiered analysis pipeline (Tier 0/1), CLI, and diagram projection. Use to implement or refactor analyzer/CLI features.

agents.name: mcp-ai-engineer
agents.role: MCP server + Claude API specialist for Archmantic. Use for the MCP server (tools over the IR), the Tier-2 LLM semantic pass, structured outputs, BYOK/model tiering, and the token-savings benchmark.

agents.name: product-strategist
agents.role: Product/competitive strategist for Archmantic. Use to pressure-test scope, sharpen USPs/positioning, validate against competitors, and keep the team out of the red ocean. Guards MVP focus.

agents.name: ui-designer
agents.role: UI/visual designer for Archmantic's web app. Use to design layout, app shell, design system (spacing/type/color/density), interactive diagram canvases, and concrete component/library choices — with ASCII mockups. Proposes and specifies; implements only when asked.

agents.name: ux-researcher
agents.role: UX researcher for Archmantic's web app. Use to audit flows, define personas/tasks/information-architecture, run heuristic evaluations, and ground design decisions in evidence + competitive patterns. Research and recommend — does not write product code.

manifest.goal: Be an always-on Solution Architect: keep a single, grounded, trustworthy architecture model that humans and AI agents share — diagrams for people, an MCP surface for agents — so the model never drifts from the code and agents read it instead of whole files.

history.note: 1.4–1.7: monorepo, NestJS, Laravel/PHP (routes, migrations, Vue/Inertia, Blade/Livewire), reliable usage outbox.

narrative: Archmantic reverse-engineers a single grounded architecture model (the IR) from a repo and serves it to both humans — interactive diagrams plus an AI-curated map — and AI agents, over MCP. A tiered, cheapest-first pipeline collects everything with file:line provenance; projections render it as context / component / sequence / ERD views and a domain map; the spec layer (project brain, features) and skills add human intent on top of the structure. The same model an agent queries is the one a human edits.

processes.description: Modules orchestrated from the src/cli.ts entry point (structural; refined by Tier 2).
processes.name: Cli flow

systems.description: Archmantic — an always-on Solution Architect: a living, grounded architecture model + diagrams for humans and AI agents (via MCP). Detects TS/JS and PHP/Laravel stacks, monorepos, API surface, and data models.
systems.name: archmantic
